'''Tunnatutikan''' was a riverside city on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].

==Description==
Tunnatutikan was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. The city was bordered by a [[river]] to the south and was hemmed in by [[Tree/Legends|forests]] on the other sides. In the time after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the city was near three [[Jammer/Legends|jammers]], one of which was located inside a large [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] installation. An Imperial supply depot was located to the south, and a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] camp was located further north up the river. The city itself featured a [[Command center]], [[prefab shelter]]s, and military buildings such as a [[Shipyard (water)|shipyard]]. A damaged [[Heavy Weapons Factory]] was located in Tunnatutikan.

==History==
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], after escaping the city of [[Okikuti]] and the Wookiee [[Weebacca]]'s trap, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] members [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], the latter of whom was a Wookiee himself, reached Tunnatutikan and met with [[Shoran]], Chewbacca's cousin and leader of [[Shoran's Defenders]]. From there, they repaired the Heavy Weapons Factory and recruited a Wookiee [[Army/Legends|army]] to destroy the three Imperial jammers in [[Chenachochan]] in order to communicate with the New Republic about the situation on Kashyyyk.

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}
Chewbacca and Solo infiltrated the supply depot and acquired the [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] stored there, as well as destroyed the Trandoshan slaver camp to free the captured [[Cruiser (Wookiee)|Heavy Cruisers]]. With those forces, supplemented by additional Wookiee [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and Heavy Weapons constructed at the factory, the Wookiee army led by Solo, Chewbacca, and Shoran knocked out the three jammers and sent out a distress signal.
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Tunnatutikan was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. It appeared and received its name during a Chewbacca campaign mission. The mission and thus the city was also included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. The size of the army is the player's choice, and freeing the captured units is an optional objective. This article assumes the player did so.
